title: Oxidative Stress Protocol
author: Wen Aw
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.r69d9h6

steps.instruction: 10 Larvae were disseted on ice and placed in 100uL of ice-cold mitochondrial isolation buffer
The larvae were homogenized by 80 strokes up and down using sterile kontes pellet pestles
The homogenate was pipette into the syringe (with 2cm2 of gauze pad), followed by 400uL of isolation buffer
Gently press through the gauze into a clean 1.5mL tube
Filtered homogenate was centrifuge at 1500g for 8 min.
The resulting brown grey pellet was washed with 200uL of isolation buffer 1 to 2 times to remove the lipid.
The final mitochondrial pellet was re-suspend in 20uL of mitochondrial isolation buffer
5uL of mitochondria solution was added into 180uL respiration buffer in 96 well plate and stirer by magnatic stirir
Add 5uL of 2.20mM ampliflu red (55uM final conc) + 10uL of 0.02U/uL(20U/mL) horseradish peroxide (1U mL-1 final conc)

After the reaction was initiated, substrate were added as follows (allowing a period of stabilization between each step (Pyruvate + L-poline + Malate each 10mM, ADP 5mM, Rotenone 0.5uM and Antimycin A 2.5uM)
